Notice Of Motion To Compel Defts Further Responses To Pltfs Request For Admissions, Set One; Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-13-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearance:
Shaana A. Rahman, appeared for Plaintiff/Moving party
James F. Hannawalt, appeared for Defendant/Opposing party S.F. Dept. of Traffic
Bridget M. Mattos, CSR #11410 (for Pltf.)
Ruling:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff Debra Hemrich's Motion To Compel Defendant City and County of San Francisco's Further Responses To Request For Admissions, Set One, is granted. The court does not find that the requests are oppressive or excessive. Responses shall be made within 15 days of notice of this order. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. Late opposition has been considered. Order signed in open court. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

Motion To Compel Deft Manuel N Angeles' Further Responses To Pltfs Special Interrogatories Set One; Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-13-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearance:
Shaana A. Rahman, appeared for Plaintiff/Moving party
James F. Hannawalt, appeared for Defendant/Opposing party S.F. Dept. of Traffic
Bridget M. Mattos, CSR #11410 (for Pltf.)
Ruling:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff Debra Hemrich's Motion To Compel Defendant Manuel Angeles' Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One, is granted as to Nos. 36-44 and 47-59. The motion is granted as to Nos. 45 and 46, limited to driving. Responses shall be made within 15 days of notice of this order. Sanctions are awarded against Defendant in the amount of $490, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. Late opposition has been considered. Order signed in open court. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

Notice Of Motion To Compel Deft Further Responses To Pltfs Request For Production Of Documents, Set One; Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-13-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Shaana A. Rahman, appeared for Plaintiff/Moving party
James F. Hannawalt, appeared for Defendant/Opposing party S.F. Dept. of Traffic
Bridget M. Mattos, CSR #11410 (for Pltf.)
Ruling:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff Debra D. Hemrich's Motion To Compel Defendant City and County of San Francisco's Further Responses To Request For Production of Documents, Set One, is granted in part and denied in part.
The motion is denied as it seeks the evaluation report by Timothy Maloney regarding Angeles, dated 6/2/2005, as it constitutes a part of the personnel file. Any discovery of the personnel file is limited to complaints relating to Defendant Manuel Angeles' driving.
The motion is granted as to the memorandum by Terrence Ng in response to plaintiff's claim, dated 5/9/2005, as no evidence has been offered in support of the claim of privilege.
The motion is denied as to the witness interviews obtained by City Attorney investigator Brian Cauley, there being no showing of good cause to obtain that qualified work product.
The motion is denied in part and granted in part as to Request No. 5 as set forth above.
The motion is denied as to Request No. 11 as it is overly broad. Any request for personnel records must be limited to complaints about Defendant Angeles' driving within a 5 year period prior to the accident.
Late opposition has been considered.
Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied.
Order signed in open court. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

Motion To Compel Defts Further Responses To Pltfs Special Interrogatories, Set One; Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-13-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearance:
Shaana A. Rahman, appeared for Plaintiff/Moving party
James F. Hannawalt, appeared for Defendant/Opposing party S.F. Dept. of Traffic
Bridget M. Mattos, CSR #11410 (for Pltf.)
Ruling:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff Debra Hemrich's Motion To Compel Defendant City and County of San Francisco's Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One, is granted as to Nos. 36-36 and 48-61. The motion is granted as to No. 47, limited to driving. Responses shall be made within 15 days of notice of this order. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. Late opposition has been considered. Order signed in open court. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

Motion To Compel Deft Further Responses To Pltfs Request For Admissions, Set One; Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-13-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearance:
Shaana A. Rahman, appeared for Plaintiff/Moving party
James F. Hannawalt, appeared for Defendant/Opposing party S.F. Dept. of Traffic
Bridget M. Mattos, CSR #11410 (for Pltf.)
Ruling:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff Debra Hemrich's Motion To Compel Defendant Manuel Angeles Further Responses To Request For Admissions, Set One, is granted. The court does not find that the requests are oppressive or excessive. Responses shall be made within 15 days of notice of this order. The court finds that the failure to answer the requests was a misuse of the discovery process. Sanctions are awarded in the amount of $490, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. Late opposition has been considered. Order signed in open court. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
